fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 69 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), which incorrectly validates tokens when the current time is exactly equal to the expiry time. The comparison should be inclusive (`>=`) to correctly handle the boundary case.

constraints.intent: A token is valid only if the current time is strictly before its adjusted expiry time.
If a token is considered valid (i.e., this branch is taken), the current time must be strictly before its expiry.
The adjusted expiry time, accounting for clock skew, should not be earlier than the original expiry time.

spec.expr: record__1 is None or (isinstance(record__1, tuple) and len(record__1) == 2 and isinstance(record__1[0], str))

constraints.intent: A retrieved token record must either be None or a tuple containing a string value and a timestamp.
